description:
You are a Site Reliability Engineer migrating alerting to Icinga while metrics remain in Graphite. Create an executable Python script at /home/ga/check_graphite_metric.py that queries a metric from Graphite's Render API (http://localhost/render) and outputs a Nagios-compliant status line and exit code. The script must use the argparse module to accept a required --target string, required --warning and --critical floats, and an optional --minutes integer (defaulting to 10). 

The script should query the API using format=json, the URL-encoded target, and a time range starting from the specified minutes ago. It must evaluate the most recent non-null data point from the first returned series against the thresholds using a greater-than-or-equal-to (>=) comparison. Output the result in the format "[STATUS] - <target> is <value> | metric=<value>;<warning>;<critical>" with the corresponding Nagios exit codes: 0 for OK (value < warning), 1 for WARNING (value >= warning), 2 for CRITICAL (value >= critical), and 3 for UNKNOWN (no data, HTTP errors, or all null values). Ensure the pipe symbol separates the human-readable text from the performance data.
